title: Rich Irish Oat Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Irish stew made with wholemeal flour and tender oats, perfect for a crowd, this sugar-free-friendly dish is cooked to perfection in a copper mug using a pressure cooker.
instructions: In a large copper mug, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the lamb or beef stew meat and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the wholemeal flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rolled oats, carrots, potatoes, broth, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Use a pressure cooker to cook the stew for an additional 20 minutes, or until the meat is tender.
Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter until melted and fully incorporated.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
